fresh_facts.claim: US military planners are preparing strikes on IRGC leaders including Ahmad Vahidi, naval assets, and energy infrastructure if Iran talks collapse.
US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth warned Iran to 'choose wisely' and confirmed global enforcement against Iranian-linked ships and readiness to counter Strait of Hormuz mining.
Three US aircraft carrier groups have been deployed as part of a strengthened naval blockade against Iran.
A 'shoot and kill' order has been issued for Iranian boats in the Strait of Hormuz if they violate restrictions.
Iran-linked proxy forces conducted drone strikes on Kuwait's northern border posts on April 24, 2026, launched from Iraq.
Kuwait's military confirmed two drones damaged border sites, attributing them to pro-Iran militias.
IRGC Quds Force commander Esmail Qaani visited Iraq, with warnings preceding the drone strikes on Kuwait.
US President Trump claimed Iran's military is 'totally defeated' with leaders 'fighting like cats.'
President Trump extended a ceasefire with Iran to allow Tehran time for a 'unified proposal.'
Mediators from the US and Pakistan await a response from Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei, expected on Wednesday.
Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, head of Iran's US negotiating team, quit amid internal disagreements.
Iranian civilian officials including the foreign minister and parliament speaker favor continuing talks, while Revolutionary Guard commanders oppose until the Strait of Hormuz issue resolves.
Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi conveyed a 'comprehensive response to proposals' during talks with Pakistan's army chief.
Internal disputes within Iranian leadership blocked negotiators' trip to Islamabad.
IRGC Navy seized two vessels in the Strait of Hormuz hours after the ceasefire extension.
IRGC-linked media mapped undersea internet cables, signaling a potential warning.
A secret letter to Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei has triggered top-level uncertainty and highlighted sensitivities over divisions within Iranian leadership.
Exiled Prince Reza Pahlavi dismissed Iranian negotiators as part of the repression system.
Bread shortages and soaring prices are straining Iranian households amid sanctions and conflict.
UK PM Keir Starmer pledged to proscribe the IRGC in upcoming legislation.
Two men accused of Iran-linked surveillance on Jewish and Israeli sites in London face court proceedings.
US envoys were heading to Pakistan for Iran talks as of April 24, 2026.
Unverified reports suggest Iran-backed Iraqi militias have been deployed to Tehran's streets for security purposes.
Iran has granted Iran-backed Iraqi militias greater autonomy amid US pressure on Iraq to curb such groups.

regime_fall.current_status: Iran faces escalating military pressure from three US carrier groups with explicit strike preparations targeting IRGC leadership and energy infrastructure, while internal divisions between negotiation-favoring civilians and hardline military commanders intensify amid a secret letter to Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei triggering top-level uncertainty. Economic deterioration including bread shortages compounds regime fragility as diplomatic channels remain blocked by internal disputes despite Trump administration ceasefire extensions.
regime_fall.last_major_event: On June 16, 2026, the IDF struck Iranian regime security institutions and the Shahran oil depot near Tehran, while Iran launched approximately 30 ballistic missiles targeting Haifa in Operation True Promise III with 92% interception rates, and Supreme Leader Khamenei appointed Maj. Gen. Amir Hatami as Artesh commander emphasizing 'active deterrence.'
